flags.concern: Routing migration: step 1 is not actually isolated. Switching `useJoinClips()` to `useNavigate()` before the BrowserRouter hoist would introduce a render-time router dependency into `useSharedLightboxState()`, and `AgentChat` currently renders `MediaLightbox` as a sibling of `AppRoutes`, outside router context. Following the stated order would therefore break the AgentChat attachment-lightbox path until step 3 lands.
flags.evidence: Reordered steps to fix FLAG-001: the BrowserRouter hoist (formerly Step 3) is now Step 1, and the useJoinClips migration (formerly Step 1) is now Step 3. This ensures useNavigate() has a router provider before useJoinClips switches to it, since AgentChat renders MediaLightbox which calls useJoinClips at render time — and AgentChat sits outside the old RouterProvider's context. Added explicit explanation of the ordering constraint in the Overview and Execution Order sections.

flags.concern: Route error handling: the plan still treats dropping `errorElement` as effectively free, but this repo uses `errorElement: <NotFoundPage />` on the public routes and the `Layout` route today. `ToolErrorBoundary` only covers tool pages, so after the migration those non-tool render failures would fall through to the app-wide `AppErrorBoundary` instead of the existing NotFoundPage/error-element behavior. That may be acceptable, but the plan needs to make that behavior change explicit or add a replacement boundary instead of leaving it as 'can be added later'.
flags.evidence: `src/app/routes.tsx:65-121` attaches `errorElement: <NotFoundPage />` to `/`, `/home`, payment/share/blog, and the `Layout` route. The only app-wide fallback today is `AppErrorBoundary`, which wraps `<App />` in `src/app/bootstrap.tsx:141-145` and renders its own generic error UI in `src/app/components/error/AppErrorBoundary.tsx:17-84`.
